name: verify_finding
description: Adversarial verification of one claimed finding: re-run its PoC request live, then a skeptical LLM judges whether the evidence + replay prove a real, reproducible vulnerability. System prompt injected from prompts/verify.md.

inputParameters.queryExpression: { finding: .finding, poc_replay: { status: (.replay.response.status // null), body_excerpt: ((.replay.response.body_excerpt // .replay.error // "<no replay>")[0:800]), summary: (.replay.summary // ""), leaked_data: (.replay.response.sensitive // {found:false}), data_classes: ((.replay.response.sensitive.classes) // {}) }, oob_confirmation: { checked: ((.finding.oob_tokens // []) | length > 0), hit: (.oob.hit // false), count: (.oob.count // 0), source_ips: (.oob.source_ips // []), user_agents: (.oob.user_agents // []), note: (if ((.finding.oob_tokens // []) | length) == 0 then "no OOB canary was planted for this finding" elif (.oob.hit // false) then "OOB collaborator received an inbound hit on the planted canary -- strong confirmation of a blind/server-side request" else "OOB canary was planted but NO inbound hit was observed -- blind vector NOT confirmed" end) } } | tojson

messages.message:
Claimed finding + live PoC replay + OOB confirmation (JSON):
${prep_verify.output.result}

inputParameters.queryExpression: .raw as $r | (if ($r|type) == "object" then $r else (try ($r[ ($r|index("{")) : (($r|rindex("}"))+1) ] | fromjson) catch {real: false, confidence: "low", reasoning: "verifier output could not be parsed"}) end)
